{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a028b59c45534e2aafdf0e1d","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t// Package simulator provides integration between simulator virtual nodes and fleet registry.\n2\tpackage simulator\n3\t\n4\timport (\n5\t\t\"fmt\"\n6\t\t\"log\"\n7\t\t\"sort\"\n8\t)\n9\t\n10\t// DefaultNodeOrigin is the degenerate position virtual nodes carry when they\n11\t// are created without an explicit placement — it matches the fleet registry's\n12\t// nodes-table schema defaults (pos_x=0, pos_y=0, pos_z=1). Nodes still sitting\n13\t// at this origin are treated as \"unset\": the bridge reassigns them spread-out\n14\t// geometry at sync time so the registry and downstream fusion engine never see\n15\t// co-located / all-at-origin virtual nodes (core symptom in bf-18yn / bf-4q5w).\n16\tvar DefaultNodeOrigin = Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: 1}\n17\t\n18\t// isDefaultOrigin reports whether p is the unset DB-default origin position.\n19\tfunc isDefaultOrigin(p Point) bool {\n20\t\treturn p.X == DefaultNodeOrigin.X && p.Y == DefaultNodeOrigin.Y && p.Z == DefaultNodeOrigin.Z\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\t// FleetRegistryBridge integrates virtual nodes with the fleet registry.\n24\t// This allows virtual nodes to participate in coverage planning and role assignment.\n25\ttype FleetRegistryBridge struct {\n26\t\tstore       *VirtualNodeStore\n27\t\tregistryKey string // Prefix for MAC addresses in registry\n28\t}\n29\t\n30\t// space returns the geometry the bridge spreads default nodes across. It\n31\t// prefers the store's configured space and falls back to DefaultSpace() so the\n32\t// bridge is safe even when the store has no space attached.\n33\tfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) space() *Space {\n34\t\tif b.store != nil {\n35\t\t\tif s := b.store.GetSpace(); s != nil {\n36\t\t\t\treturn s\n37\t\t\t}\n38\t\t}\n39\t\treturn DefaultSpace()\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\t// effectivePositions returns the registry position to sync for each node ID.\n43\t// Nodes still at the default DB origin (DefaultNodeOrigin) are reassigned\n44\t// distinct, spread-out geometry from DefaultNodePositions — sized to the full\n45\t// node count so even when every node is unset the geometry spans the room —\n46\t// while explicitly-placed (non-origin) nodes keep their position. Default-origin\n47\t// nodes are assigned successive spread slots in sorted-ID order, so the mapping\n48\t// is deterministic regardless of map iteration order and a single-node sync\n49\t// produces the same position as a full sync. The result therefore has no two\n50\t// co-located points and is never entirely at the origin.\n51\tfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) effectivePositions(nodes []*VirtualNodeState) map[string]Point {\n52\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Computing effective positions for %d virtual nodes\", len(nodes))\n53\t\teffective := make(map[string]Point, len(nodes))\n54\t\tvar defaults []*VirtualNodeState\n55\t\tfor _, n := range nodes {\n56\t\t\tif isDefaultOrigin(n.Position) {\n57\t\t\t\tdefaults = append(defaults, n)\n58\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Node %s at default origin (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f) - will be reassigned\", n.ID, n.Position.X, n.Position.Y, n.Position.Z)\n59\t\t\t} else {\n60\t\t\t\teffective[n.ID] = n.Position\n61\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Node %s keeping explicit position (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f)\", n.ID, n.Position.X, n.Position.Y, n.Position.Z)\n62\t\t\t}\n63\t\t}\n64\t\tif len(defaults) == 0 {\n65\t\t\treturn effective\n66\t\t}\n67\t\n68\t\t// Deterministic slot assignment independent of map iteration order.\n69\t\tsort.Slice(defaults, func(i, j int) bool { return defaults[i].ID < defaults[j].ID })\n70\t\n71\t\tspace := b.space()\n72\t\tspread := DefaultNodePositions(space, len(nodes))\n73\t\n74\t\t// Track positions already in use so default-origin nodes never collide\n75\t\t// with each other or with an explicitly-placed node.\n76\t\ttaken := make(map[Point]bool, len(effective)+len(defaults))\n77\t\tfor _, p := range effective {\n78\t\t\ttaken[p] = true\n79\t\t}\n80\t\n81\t\tsi := 0\n82\t\tfor _, n := range defaults {\n83\t\t\tfor si < len(spread) && taken[spread[si]] {\n84\t\t\t\tsi++\n85\t\t\t}\n86\t\t\tvar pos Point\n87\t\t\tif si < len(spread) {\n88\t\t\t\tpos = spread[si]\n89\t\t\t\tsi++\n90\t\t\t} else {\n91\t\t\t\t// Spread set exhausted: more default nodes than slots. The set is\n92\t\t\t\t// sized to len(nodes) so this is effectively unreachable; fall back\n93\t\t\t\t// to the room center, which is still distinct from the origin for\n94\t\t\t\t// any non-degenerate room.\n95\t\t\t\tminX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n96\t\t\t\tpos = Point{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: (minZ + maxZ) / 2}\n97\t\t\t}\n98\t\t\teffective[n.ID] = pos\n99\t\t\ttaken[pos] = true\n100\t\t}\n101\t\n102\t\treturn effective\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t// NewFleetRegistryBridge creates a new bridge between virtual nodes and fleet registry\n106\tfunc NewFleetRegistryBridge(store *VirtualNodeStore) *FleetRegistryBridge {\n107\t\treturn &FleetRegistryBridge{\n108\t\t\tstore:       store,\n109\t\t\tregistryKey: \"virtual\",\n110\t\t}\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// RegistryNodeAdapter is an interface for fleet registry operations\n114\ttype RegistryNodeAdapter interface {\n115\t\tAddVirtualNode(mac, name string, x, y, z float64) error\n116\t\tSetNodePosition(mac string, x, y, z float64) error\n117\t\tSetNodeRole(mac, role string) error\n118\t\tDeleteNode(mac string) error\n119\t\tGetNode(mac string) (*NodeRecord, error)\n120\t\tGetAllNodes() ([]NodeRecord, error)\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\t// NodeRecord represents a node record from the fleet registry\n124\ttype NodeRecord struct {\n125\t\tMAC     string\n126\t\tName    string\n127\t\tRole    string\n128\t\tPosX    float64\n129\t\tPosY    float64\n130\t\tPosZ    float64\n131\t\tVirtual bool\n132\t\tEnabled bool\n133\t}\n134\t\n135\t// SyncToRegistry synchronizes all virtual nodes to the fleet registry.\n136\t//\n137\t// Positions are resolved through effectivePositions: any node still at the\n138\t// default DB origin (DefaultNodeOrigin) is reassigned distinct, spread-out\n139\t// geometry across the store's space so the registry — and the fusion engine\n140\t// fed from it via the existing wiring — never observes co-located or\n141\t// all-at-origin virtual nodes.\n142\tfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) SyncToRegistry(registry RegistryNodeAdapter) error {\n143\t\tif registry == nil {\n144\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"registry is nil\")\n145\t\t}\n146\t\n147\t\tnodes := b.store.ListNodes()\n148\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Registry bridge syncing %d virtual nodes to fleet registry\", positions[node.ID]\n154\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Syncing node %s (MAC: %s) to position (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f)\", node.ID, mac, pos.X, pos.Y, pos.Z)\n155\t\n156\t\t\t// Check if node exists in Positions\n296\t// are resolved through effectivePositions so the records reflect exactly what\n297\t// SyncToRegistry would write (spread geometry for default-origin nodes).\n298\tfunc (b Recommended number of nodes\n329\t\tSuggestedPositions []Point `json:\"suggested_positions\"` // Suggested positions for new nodes\n330\t\tWeakAreas          []Point `json:\"weak_areas\"`          // Areas with poor coverage\n331\t\tImprovementDelta   float64 `json:\"improvement_delta\"`   // Expected improvement with suggestions\n332\t}\n333\t\n334\t// OptimizeCoverage analyzes current coverage and suggests improvements\n335\tfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) OptimizeCoverage(space *Space) (*CoverageOptimization, error) {\n336\t\tnodeSet := b.store.ToNodeSet()\n337\t\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodeSet)\n338\t\n339\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n340\t\n341\t\tconfig := GridConfig{\n342\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n343\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n344\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n345\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n346\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n347\t\t}\n348\t\n349\t\tgc := NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n350\t\tresults := gc.ComputeAll()\n351\t\n352\t\tcurrentScore := gc.CoverageScore(results)\n353\t\n354\t\t// Find weak areas (cells with GDOP > 4)\n355\t\tweakAreas := make([]Point, 0)\n356\t\tfor row := range results {\n357\t\t\tfor col := range results[row] {\n358\t\t\t\tif results[row][col].GDOP > 4 {\n359\t\t\t\t\t// Calculate position from grid indices\n360\t\t\t\t\tx := minX + float64(col)*config.CellSize\n361\t\t\t\t\ty := minY + float64(row)*config.CellSize\n362\t\t\t\t\tweakAreas = append(weakAreas, Point{X: x, Y: y, Z: 1.5})\n363\t\t\t\t}\n364\t\t\t}\n365\t\t}\n366\t\n367\t\t// Suggest positions based on corner placement strategy\n368\t\tsuggestedPositions := CornerPositions(space)\n369\t\n370\t\t// Estimate improvement with suggested nodes\n371\t\t// This is a heuristic - in practice, you'd simulate with the suggested nodes\n372\t\timprovementDelta := 100.0 - currentScore\n373\t\tif improvementDelta > 50 {\n374\t\t\timprovementDelta = 50 // Cap at 50% expected improvement\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\treturn &CoverageOptimization{\n378\t\t\tCurrentScore:       currentScore,\n379\t\t\tRecommendedNodes:   MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n380\t\t\tSuggestedPositions: suggestedPositions,\n381\t\t\tWeakAreas: Package simulator provides integration between simulator virtual nodes and fleet registry.\npackage simulator\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"sort\"\n)\n\n// DefaultNodeOrigin is the degenerate position virtual nodes carry when they\n// are created without an explicit placement — it matches the fleet registry's\n// nodes-table schema defaults (pos_x=0, pos_y=0, pos_z=1). Nodes still sitting\n// at this origin are treated as \"unset\": the bridge reassigns them spread-out\n// geometry at sync time so the registry and downstream fusion engine never see\n// co-located / all-at-origin virtual nodes (core symptom in bf-18yn / bf-4q5w).\nvar DefaultNodeOrigin = Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: 1}\n\n// isDefaultOrigin reports whether p is the unset DB-default origin position.\nfunc isDefaultOrigin(p Point) bool {\n\treturn p.X == DefaultNodeOrigin.X && p.Y == DefaultNodeOrigin.Y && p.Z == DefaultNodeOrigin.Z\n}\n\n// FleetRegistryBridge integrates virtual nodes with the fleet registry.\n// This allows virtual nodes to participate in coverage planning and role assignment.\ntype FleetRegistryBridge struct {\n\tstore       *VirtualNodeStore\n\tregistryKey string // Prefix for MAC addresses in registry\n}\n\n// space returns the geometry the bridge spreads default nodes across. It\n// prefers the store's configured space and falls back to DefaultSpace() so the\n// bridge is safe even when the store has no space attached.\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) space() *Space {\n\tif b.store != nil {\n\t\tif s := b.store.GetSpace(); s != nil {\n\t\t\treturn s\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn DefaultSpace()\n}\n\n// effectivePositions returns the registry position to sync for each node ID.\n// Nodes still at the default DB origin (DefaultNodeOrigin) are reassigned\n// distinct, spread-out geometry from DefaultNodePositions — sized to the full\n// node count so even when every node is unset the geometry spans the room —\n// while explicitly-placed (non-origin) nodes keep their position. Default-origin\n// nodes are assigned successive spread slots in sorted-ID order, so the mapping\n// is deterministic regardless of map iteration order and a single-node sync\n// produces the same position as a full sync. The result therefore has no two\n// co-located points and is never entirely at the origin.\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) effectivePositions(nodes []*VirtualNodeState) map[string]Point {\n\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Computing effective positions for %d virtual nodes\", len(nodes))\n\teffective := make(map[string]Point, len(nodes))\n\tvar defaults []*VirtualNodeState\n\tfor _, n := range nodes {\n\t\tif isDefaultOrigin(n.Position) {\n\t\t\tdefaults = append(defaults, n)\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Node %s at default origin (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f) - will be reassigned\", n.ID, n.Position.X, n.Position.Y, n.Position.Z)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\teffective[n.ID] = n.Position\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Node %s keeping explicit position (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f)\", n.ID, n.Position.X, n.Position.Y, n.Position.Z)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif len(defaults) == 0 {\n\t\treturn effective\n\t}\n\n\t// Deterministic slot assignment independent of map iteration order.\n\tsort.Slice(defaults, func(i, j int) bool { return defaults[i].ID < defaults[j].ID })\n\n\tspace := b.space()\n\tspread := DefaultNodePositions(space, len(nodes))\n\n\t// Track positions already in use so default-origin nodes never collide\n\t// with each other or with an explicitly-placed node.\n\ttaken := make(map[Point]bool, len(effective)+len(defaults))\n\tfor _, p := range effective {\n\t\ttaken[p] = true\n\t}\n\n\tsi := 0\n\tfor _, n := range defaults {\n\t\tfor si < len(spread) && taken[spread[si]] {\n\t\t\tsi++\n\t\t}\n\t\tvar pos Point\n\t\tif si < len(spread) {\n\t\t\tpos = spread[si]\n\t\t\tsi++\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Spread set exhausted: more default nodes than slots. The set is\n\t\t\t// sized to len(nodes) so this is effectively unreachable; fall back\n\t\t\t// to the room center, which is still distinct from the origin for\n\t\t\t// any non-degenerate room.\n\t\t\tminX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n\t\t\tpos = Point{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: (minZ + maxZ) / 2}\n\t\t}\n\t\teffective[n.ID] = pos\n\t\ttaken[pos] = true\n\t}\n\n\treturn effective\n}\n\n// NewFleetRegistryBridge creates a new bridge between virtual nodes and fleet registry\nfunc NewFleetRegistryBridge(store *VirtualNodeStore) *FleetRegistryBridge {\n\treturn &FleetRegistryBridge{\n\t\tstore:       store,\n\t\tregistryKey: \"virtual\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// RegistryNodeAdapter is an interface for fleet registry operations\ntype RegistryNodeAdapter interface {\n\tAddVirtualNode(mac, name string, x, y, z float64) error\n\tSetNodePosition(mac string, x, y, z float64) error\n\tSetNodeRole(mac, role string) error\n\tDeleteNode(mac string) error\n\tGetNode(mac string) (*NodeRecord, error)\n\tGetAllNodes() ([]NodeRecord, error)\n}\n\n// NodeRecord represents a node record from the fleet registry\ntype NodeRecord struct {\n\tMAC     string\n\tName    string\n\tRole    string\n\tPosX    float64\n\tPosY    float64\n\tPosZ    float64\n\tVirtual bool\n\tEnabled bool\n}\n\n// SyncToRegistry synchronizes all virtual nodes to the fleet registry.\n//\n// Positions are resolved through effectivePositions: any node still at the\n// default DB origin (DefaultNodeOrigin) is reassigned distinct, spread-out\n// geometry across the store's space so the registry — and the fusion engine\n// fed from it via the existing wiring — never observes co-located or\n// all-at-origin virtual nodes.\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) SyncToRegistry(registry RegistryNodeAdapter) error {\n\tif registry == nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"registry is nil\")\n\t}\n\n\tnodes := b.store.ListNodes()\n\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Registry bridge syncing %d virtual nodes to fleet registry\", len(nodes))\n\tpositions := b.effectivePositions(nodes)\n\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tmac := b.virtualMAC(node.ID)\n\t\tpos := positions[node.ID]\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Syncing node %s (MAC: %s) to position (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f)\", node.ID, mac, pos.X, pos.Y, pos.Z)\n\n\t\t// Check if node exists in registry\n\t\texisting, err := registry.GetNode(mac)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// Node doesn't exist, create it\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Creating new virtual node %s in registry at (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f)\", node.ID, pos.X, pos.Y, pos.Z)\n\t\t\tif err := registry.AddVirtualNode(\n\t\t\t\tmac,\n\t\t\t\tnode.Name,\n\t\t\t\tpos.X,\n\t\t\t\tpos.Y,\n\t\t\t\tpos.Z,\n\t\t\t); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"add virtual node %s: %w\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Node exists, update position/role if changed\n\t\t\tif existing.PosX != pos.X ||\n\t\t\t\texisting.PosY != pos.Y ||\n\t\t\t\texisting.PosZ != pos.Z {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Updating position for node %s from (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f) to (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f)\",\n\t\t\t\t\tnode.ID, existing.PosX, existing.PosY, existing.PosZ, pos.X, pos.Y, pos.Z)\n\t\t\t\tif err := registry.SetNodePosition(mac,\n\t\t\t\t\tpos.X,\n\t\t\t\t\tpos.Y,\n\t\t\t\t\tpos.Z,\n\t\t\t\t); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"update position for %s: %w\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif existing.Role != string(node.Role) {\n\t\t\t\tif err := registry.SetNodeRole(mac, string(node.Role)); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"update role for %s: %w\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// TODO: Remove registry nodes that no longer exist in virtual store?\n\t// For now, we keep them to avoid accidentally deleting user data\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// SyncOneNode syncs a single virtual node to the registry.\n//\n// The effective position is resolved over the full node set (not just this\n// node) so a single-node sync produces the same spread geometry a full sync\n// would write: default-origin nodes receive deterministic spread slots keyed\n// by their rank among the unset nodes.\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) SyncOneNode(registry RegistryNodeAdapter, nodeID string) error {\n\tif registry == nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"registry is nil\")\n\t}\n\n\tnode, err := b.store.GetNode(nodeID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"get node %s: %w\", nodeID, err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Resolve over the full set so the slot assignment matches SyncToRegistry.\n\tpositions := b.effectivePositions(b.store.ListNodes())\n\tpos := positions[nodeID]\n\n\tmac := b.virtualMAC(nodeID)\n\n\texisting, err := registry.GetNode(mac)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Node doesn't exist, create it\n\t\treturn registry.AddVirtualNode(\n\t\t\tmac,\n\t\t\tnode.Name,\n\t\t\tpos.X,\n\t\t\tpos.Y,\n\t\t\tpos.Z,\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\n\t// Update existing node\n\tif existing.PosX != pos.X ||\n\t\texisting.PosY != pos.Y ||\n\t\texisting.PosZ != pos.Z {\n\t\tif err := registry.SetNodePosition(mac,\n\t\t\tpos.X,\n\t\t\tpos.Y,\n\t\t\tpos.Z,\n\t\t); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"update position: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif existing.Role != string(node.Role) {\n\t\tif err := registry.SetNodeRole(mac, string(node.Role)); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"update role: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// RemoveFromRegistry removes a virtual node from the fleet registry\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) RemoveFromRegistry(registry RegistryNodeAdapter, nodeID string) error {\n\tif registry == nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"registry is nil\")\n\t}\n\n\tmac := b.virtualMAC(nodeID)\n\treturn registry.DeleteNode(mac)\n}\n\n// virtualMAC generates a MAC address for a virtual node\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) virtualMAC(nodeID string) string {\n\t// Hash the string nodeID into a uint32 for MAC generation\n\tvar h uint32\n\tfor _, c := range []byte(nodeID) {\n\t\th = h*31 + uint32(c)\n\t}\n\t// Use a predictable MAC pattern for virtual nodes\n\t// Format: VE:II:II:II:II where II is node ID hash bytes\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"VE:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X\",\n\t\t(h>>24)&0xFF,\n\t\t(h>>16)&0xFF,\n\t\t(h>>8)&0xFF,\n\t\th&0xFF)\n}\n\n// VirtualNodeID extracts the virtual node ID from a virtual MAC address\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) VirtualNodeID(mac string) (string, bool) {\n\t// Check if this is a virtual MAC (starts with \"VE:\")\n\tif len(mac) < 3 || mac[0:2] != \"VE\" {\n\t\treturn \"\", false\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the MAC to get the node ID hash\n\t// This is a simplified version - in practice, you'd want\n\t// a more robust bidirectional mapping\n\treturn \"\", true // TODO: implement reverse mapping\n}\n\n// ToRegistryRecords converts virtual nodes to fleet registry records. Positions\n// are resolved through effectivePositions so the records reflect exactly what\n// SyncToRegistry would write (spread geometry for default-origin nodes).\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) ToRegistryRecords() []NodeRecord {\n\tnodes := b.store.ListNodes()\n\tpositions := b.effectivePositions(nodes)\n\trecords := make([]NodeRecord, 0, len(nodes))\n\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tpos := positions[node.ID]\n\t\trecords = append(records, NodeRecord{\n\t\t\tMAC:     b.virtualMAC(node.ID),\n\t\t\tName:    node.Name,\n\t\t\tRole:    string(node.Role),\n\t\t\tPosX:    pos.X,\n\t\t\tPosY:    pos.Y,\n\t\t\tPosZ:    pos.Z,\n\t\t\tVirtual: true,\n\t\t\tEnabled: node.Enabled,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn records\n}\n\n// GetStore returns the underlying virtual node store\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) GetStore() *VirtualNodeStore {\n\treturn b.store\n}\n\n// CoverageOptimization represents optimization suggestions for virtual node placement\ntype CoverageOptimization struct {\n\tCurrentScore       float64 `json:\"current_score\"`       // Current coverage score (0-100)\n\tRecommendedNodes   int     `json:\"recommended_nodes\"`   // Recommended number of nodes\n\tSuggestedPositions []Point `json:\"suggested_positions\"` // Suggested positions for new nodes\n\tWeakAreas          []Point `json:\"weak_areas\"`          // Areas with poor coverage\n\tImprovementDelta   float64 `json:\"improvement_delta\"`   // Expected improvement with suggestions\n}\n\n// OptimizeCoverage analyzes current coverage and suggests improvements\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) OptimizeCoverage(space *Space) (*CoverageOptimization, error) {\n\tnodeSet := b.store.ToNodeSet()\n\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodeSet)\n\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\n\tconfig := GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t}\n\n\tgc := NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n\tresults := gc.ComputeAll()\n\n\tcurrentScore := gc.CoverageScore(results)\n\n\t// Find weak areas (cells with GDOP > 4)\n\tweakAreas := make([]Point, 0)\n\tfor row := range results {\n\t\tfor col := range results[row] {\n\t\t\tif results[row][col].GDOP > 4 {\n\t\t\t\t// Calculate position from grid indices\n\t\t\t\tx := minX + float64(col)*config.CellSize\n\t\t\t\ty := minY + float64(row)*config.CellSize\n\t\t\t\tweakAreas = append(weakAreas, Point{X: x, Y: y, Z: 1.5})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Suggest positions based on corner placement strategy\n\tsuggestedPositions := CornerPositions(space)\n\n\t// Estimate improvement with suggested nodes\n\t// This is a heuristic - in practice, you'd simulate with the suggested nodes\n\timprovementDelta := 100.0 - currentScore\n\tif improvementDelta > 50 {\n\t\timprovementDelta = 50 // Cap at 50% expected improvement\n\t}\n\n\treturn &CoverageOptimization{\n\t\tCurrentScore:       currentScore,\n\t\tRecommendedNodes:   MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n\t\tSuggestedPositions: suggestedPositions,\n\t\tWeakAreas:          weakAreas,\n\t\tImprovementDelta:   improvementDelta,\n\t}, nil\n}\n","numLines":385,"startLine":1,"totalLines":385}}}

A blob there is only\n+// possible if BOTH positions reached the accumulation grid (Fuse's\n+// AddLinkInfluence reads nodePos[mac] for both endpoints; a missing or\n+// co-located position produces no peak — see TestEngine_CoLocatedOriginYieldsNoPeaks).\n+// That is the demonstrable \"positions reach the fusion engine accumulation grid\"\n+// verification this bead requires.\n+func TestPositionPropagation_ReachesAccumulationGrid(t *testing.T) {\n+\t// Full production chain, exactly as cmd/mothership/main.go wires it.\n+\treg := newFleetRegistry(t)\n+\tmgr := fleet.NewManager(reg)\n+\tengine := fusion.NewEngine(nil)\n+\tmgr.SetNodePositionSink(func(mac string, x, y, z float64) {\n+\t\tengine.SetNodePosition(mac, x, y, z)\n+\t})\n+\tadapter := &fleetRegistryTestAdapter{\n+\t\treg:        reg,\n+\t\tforwardPos: mgr.ForwardNodePosition,\n+\t}\n+\th := newWiredHandler(t, adapter)\n+\n+\t// Two explicit, in-room positions forming a horizontal link. Their midpoint\n+\t// is the unambiguous blob site a single link localizes to. Neither is at the\n+\t// default origin, so they pass through the bridge unchanged. Coordinates are\n+\t// kept inside both the simulator's DefaultSpace (6×5×2.5) and the engine's\n+\t// default grid (10×3×10): X is the long floor axis, Y/Z shared.\n+\tposA := simulator.Point{X: 0.5, Y: 1.0, Z: 1.0}\n+\tposB := simulator.Point{X: 5.5, Y: 1.0, Z: 1.0}\n+\tmidX, midZ := (posA.X+posB.X)/2, (posA.Z+posB.Z)/2\n+\n+\tpostNode(t, h, map[string]interface{}{\n+\t\t\"id\":   \"node-A\",\n+\t\t\"name\": \"Node A\",\n+\t\t\"position\": map[string]interface{}{\"x\": posA.X, \"y\": posA.Y, \"z\": posA.Z},\n+\t})\n+\tpostNode(t, h, map[string]interface{}{\n+\t\t\"id\":   \"node-B\",\n+\t\t\"name\": \"Node B\",\n+\t\t\"position\": map[string]interface{}{\"x\": posB.X, \"y\": posB.Y, \"z\": posB.Z},\n+\t})\n+\n+\t// Both simulator-created nodes must be positioned in the engine mirror.\n+\tpositions := engine.NodePositions()\n+\tif len(positions) != 2 {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected 2 positioned nodes in engine, got %d: %+v\", len(positions), positions)\n+\t}\n+\t// The bridge's virtualMAC is unexported; read the actual MACs the engine holds.\n+\tmacA, macB := positions[0].MAC, positions[1].MAC\n+\n+\t// One active link between the two simulator-created nodes. A peak near the\n+\t// midpoint forms only if both endpoints' positions are in the grid lookup.\n+\tlinks := []fusion.LinkMotion{\n+\t\t{NodeMAC: macA, PeerMAC: macB, DeltaRMS: 1.0, Motion: true},\n+\t}\n+\tresult := engine.Fuse(links)\n+\n+\tif result.ActiveLinks == 0 {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Fuse reported 0 active links — an endpoint position never reached the grid lookup\")\n+\t}\n+\tif len(result.Blobs) == 0 {\n+\t\tt.Fatalf(\"no blob localized from the link — positions did not reach the accumulation grid\")\n+\t}\n+\ttop := result.Blobs[0]\n+\t// A single horizontal link localizes its peak near the midpoint (mirrors the\n+\t// tolerance used by TestEngine_SingleLink_MidpointPeak).\n+\tif math.Abs(top.X-midX) > 1.5 {\n+\t\tt.Errorf(\"peak X %.2f not near link midpoint %.2f\", top.X, midX)\n+\t}\n+\tif math.Abs(top.Z-midZ) > 1.5 {\n+\t\tt.Errorf(\"peak Z %.2f not near link midpoint %.2f\", top.Z, midZ)\n+\t}\n+\tt.Logf(\"[grid] position propagation  midpoint=(%.2f,%.2f)  peak=(%.2f,%.2f,%.2f) conf=%.3f activeLinks=%d\",\n+\t\tmidX, midZ, top.X, top.Y, top.Z, top.Confidence, result.ActiveLinks)\n+}","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c72eec34-e220-4272-96fc-562344debbcd","uuid":"31f9c62f-b28f-4f5b-939c-52a5edcfa4de","timestamp":"2026-07-06T17:37:55.764Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"    blob there is only reachable if both endpoints' positions are in the grid\n    lookup -- AddLinkInfluence reads nodePos[mac] for both endpoints, and a\n    missing/co-located position yields no peak\n    (cf. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_9f32b71cb853491d82d742aa","type":"tool_result","content":"---\nname: auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid\ndescription: \"Auto-dispatch bead templates say `br close <id> --body`, but that arg is invalid — use comments add + close\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n  originSessionId: 2d260119-d2dc-4d0d-8b73-c865556c7fc4\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` dispatch footer instructs: `br close <id> --body \"brief summary\"`. bead-forge's `br close` does NOT accept `--body` (only `--reason`, default \"Completed\"). Blindly running the templated command fails with `unexpected argument '--body'` and climbs the bead's `failure-count` (bf-2fz8 had failure-count:2 for exactly this reason).\n\n**Why:** The dispatch template's close syntax is wrong for this `br` build; it is not the same failure mode as the historical [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]] timestamp corruption (that one errored \"premature end of input\"; bf-2fz8's `br close` ran cleanly once the body was attached separately).\n\n**How to apply:** When closing an auto-dispatched bead, attach the summary as a comment first, then close with no body flag:\n1. `br comments add <id> \"<summary text>\"`  (variadic positional TEXT — run from the repo root where `.beads/` lives, or it errors \"No .beads directory\")\n2. `br close <id>`\n3. `br sync --flush-only` (mutations don't auto-flush; checkpoint the close)\n\nAcceptance criteria phrased as \"record the finding as a bead comment\" are satisfied by step 1.\n---\n---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments. `sqlite3` binary is not installed here; use `python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"`.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c72eec34-e220-4272-96fc-562344debbcd","uuid":"b7178614-53cb-4f37-b1a0-08137b181942","timestamp":"2026-07-06T17:39:38.656Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"---\nname: auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid\ndescription: \"Auto-dispatch bead templates say `br close <id> --body`, but that arg is invalid — use comments add + close\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n  originSessionId: 2d260119-d2dc-4d0d-8b73-c865556c7fc4\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` dispatch footer instructs: `br close <id> --body \"brief summary\"`. bead-forge's `br close` does NOT accept `--body` (only `--reason`, default \"Completed\").
